scenarios.title: Common Local Viewing Baseline
scenarios.description: Use the same fully local DICOM series in both viewers. This is the clean apples-to-apples benchmark.

first_image_visible_ms.reason: Open-to-first-frame responsiveness is directly user-visible in both apps.

set_slice_present_p95_ms.reason: Tail latency during browsing determines whether the stack feels clinically usable.

cpu_p95_pct.reason: Higher CPU under the same scripted steps usually means orchestration overhead.

rss_peak_mb.reason: Peak memory reflects cache discipline and unnecessary retention.

thread_count_p95.reason: More live actors often means more coordination overhead and less calm ownership.

read_mb_delta.reason: Useful for understanding warm reopen and on-demand retrieval behavior.

write_mb_delta.reason: Useful for understanding cache persistence and extra churn.

decode_p95_ms.reason: Internal FAST path metric available from headless AI-PACS runs.

frame_render_p95_ms.reason: Internal FAST path metric available from headless AI-PACS runs.

cache_hit_ratio_pct.reason: Measures whether FAST prefetch and cache policy help the actual browsing path.

slow_frame_count_16ms.reason: Counts interactive frames that miss the 16ms budget.

longest_ui_gap_ms.reason: Tracks event-loop instability under the scripted run.

stale_task_ratio.reason: High stale work means the system is decoding work the user no longer needs.

steps.instruction: Launch the viewer and open the target series. Wait until the first image is visible.
Scroll forward steadily through the stack.
Do one fast wheel burst through a dense part of the series.
Reverse direction repeatedly so stale work and redraw discipline are stressed.
Stop all input and let the viewer settle.
Close and reopen the same series to measure warm reopen behavior.

scenarios.title: AI-PACS Live Download Overlap
scenarios.description: AI-PACS-only stress run. Use the real app with active study download, or the headless runner with simulated download workers.

set_slice_present_p95_ms.reason: This is the core overlap smoothness KPI for the user.

stack_drag_decode_hitch_count.reason: Foreground exact decode during active stack drag is a direct signature of cache-horizon misses.

stack_drag_nondecode_hitch_count.reason: Separates main-thread/UI hitches from lazy decode misses during stack drag.

cpu_p95_pct.reason: Overlap storms are visible today as CPU-heavy control-plane churn.

terminal_completion_duplicate_count.reason: Terminal completion should be one-shot per series epoch.

cache_warm_duplicate_count.reason: Post-completion cache warm must not fan out repeatedly.

stale_task_ratio.reason: High stale work means overlap logic is doing unnecessary background work.

longest_ui_gap_ms.reason: Large UI gaps are the best direct symptom of the workstation storm.

steps.instruction: Start a study download, then open the target series while the download is still active.
Scroll aggressively while download progress, thumbnails, and progressive growth are active.
Reverse direction repeatedly during the same overlap window.
Stop input and wait for settle, completion, and any post-completion cache warm to fire.

scenarios.title: AI-PACS Stack Drag Policy Comparison
scenarios.description: Compare adaptive versus ClearCanvas-style stack drag using the real QtSliceViewer and QtViewerBridge path.

set_slice_present_p95_ms.goal: lower is better, but interpret alongside traversal amount
set_slice_present_p95_ms.reason: Tail latency matters, but a policy that traverses fewer slices can look artificially faster.

stack_drag_decode_hitch_count.reason: A stack policy that drives repeated foreground decode misses is not clinically smooth even if average latency looks acceptable.

stack_drag_nondecode_hitch_count.reason: Distinguishes cache misses from UI/main-thread stalls while using the real Qt drag path.

cpu_p95_pct.reason: Shows the cost of the policy while using the actual Qt drag path.

steps.instruction: Open the target series and render the first image.
Simulate a medium stack drag using the real Qt viewer path.
Simulate a faster stack drag using the real Qt viewer path.
Stop input and let the viewer settle to the exact final slice.

scenarios.title: ClearCanvas Background Copy Pressure Approximation
scenarios.description: Approximate stress-only scenario for ClearCanvas. Open the same fully local dataset while a separate staged DICOM copy creates external disk and CPU pressure. This is not equivalent to AI-PACS live progressive growth.

first_image_visible_ms.reason: Measure cold-open behavior under external disk pressure.

set_slice_present_p95_ms.reason: Used as a stress comparison, not a direct progressive-download equivalent.

cpu_p95_pct.reason: Shows how much process cost appears under external pressure.

rss_peak_mb.reason: Shows retained working set under stress.

thread_count_p95.reason: Shows concurrent actor count under stress.

steps.instruction: Start the background staged copy, then open the target series in ClearCanvas.
Run a fast scroll burst while the background copy is still active.
Reverse direction repeatedly while the background copy remains active.
Stop input and wait for the background copy and UI activity to settle.
